mappings.rationaleAI: Architecture from ancient Egypt through modernism addresses elements of design (A.1), building materials and constructional systems (A.2), ornamentation (A.4), diverse building types (A.5), and the history of architecture (A.7).

Modern architecture covering steel, concrete, and new technologies addresses aesthetic aspects of building materials (A.2). Key buildings as cultural symbols address commercial and industrial architecture (A.5.f). The history of Western architecture (A.7.a).
British architecture covering domestic architecture and cultural identity addresses residential architecture (A.5.a) and the history of Western architecture (A.7.a).
Landscape architecture encompassing parks, gardens, and urban environments directly addresses garden and landscape design (B).
Global historical survey of gardens across cultures directly addresses garden and landscape design (B).
Urban planning covering metropolitan growth and resilience addresses garden and landscape design in the context of urban design (B).
Structural engineering covering beams, bridges, concrete, and timber addresses aesthetic aspects of building materials (A.2) and constructional systems (A.2.b).
Materials science covering metals, ceramics, and polymers addresses building materials (A.2.a).
Aesthetics examining beauty and aesthetic experience addresses elements of design and principles of composition in architecture (A.1).
Egyptian monumental building addresses religious and commemorative architecture (A.5.b) and the architecture of non-Western peoples (A.7.b).
Byzantine architecture including Hagia Sophia addresses religious architecture (A.5.b) and the history of Western architecture (A.7.a).
Islam covering mosques and religious architecture addresses religious architecture (A.5.b) and architecture of non-Western peoples (A.7.b).
Suburban development covering housing patterns and urban design addresses domestic architecture (A.5.a) and landscape design within urban contexts (B).
Sustainability covering material and energy flows in production addresses building materials and sustainability (A.2) and landscape design in the context of environmental change (B).
